2 | Issue No. 1
By Russell Brunson, the Overnight Success Maker
Over the past eight months I have done more webinars
than in the prior 12 years of my marketing career
combined. Why? Well, we have something that works.
“But Russell, why don’t you automate it...?”
Because we make changes immediately after each live
webinar we do, either to the script, the pitch, the offer, the
funnel, the email sequence, etc, based on
live feedback
from the audience.
Sure, I could have done one webinar and then automated it,
but I’m growing a company. And based on early estimates,
ClickFunnels is already getting close to a nine figure valuation
in less than eight months, so it’s worth the extra effort.
With that said, I’ve learned
so much about the funnel, that
I wanted to show off... share some of these things with you
today. J
First, the Webinar
So, I’ve pretty much showed everything about my webinar
pitch to all DCS Labs members, so you can look back in the
archives to find the perfect webinar script, or get your own
copy at www.PerfectWebinarSecrets.com. While you’re there,
I recommend buying the upsell for two reasons: first, I make
money, which is pretty cool and second, in the upsell videos
I go beyond the script and show you
how to pitch on the
webinar - very important.
With that said, I’ll move on from the webinar script, because
it’s perfect and you should just use it – it works.
Second, Multiple Landing Pages
In a perfect world, I’d push all of my traffic through the
same landing page. But, because of different types of traffic
sources, regulations at different ad networks, and different
markets that we’re going after, we have multiple landing
pages, with different messages to get people onto the
same webinar Below are a few that you may have seen.
Notice some may look similar, but have different copy,
while others have the same copy, but are displayed in
different modalities (video vs. text):
Funnel Hacks Landing Page Examples
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 11 --

DotComSecrets Labs | 5
webinar FUNNEL
Third, Confirmation Page
A lot of people who have been doing webinars have
struggled to get people to actually show up. The process of
getting them to show up starts on the confirmation page.
Here are a few things we’ve learned from our tests that
have helped a lot:
• 	Personal Video From Me Doing Four Things: First,
thanking them, second, showing them how excited I am,
third, tell them what they should expect next, fourth,
giving them something to do (aka,
buy something).
• 	Show An Event Ticket: We list all of the information
about their event, and give them the ability to re-register
(a lot of people don’t get their registration emails or
want to have another email address, so we give them
the ability to re-signup on this page.
• 	Get Them To BUY Something: I know that you’ve just
met this future attendee for the first time, but it’s
important that you get them used to you asking them
for money and them giving you money. Often what
you sell on this page will completely cover any and all
ad costs (which is
huge) and it also puts them in a state
where they can and will buy from you in the future.
Fourth, Indoctrination Pages
Between the times they register for your webinar and when
they show up, you
have to keep them engaged. We have
three videos we send out, each one telling part of a story and
getting them more excited for the live event.
If you’ve seen mine, it’s a story we are telling about an author
who is trying to create a sales funnel in ClickFunnels versus
one of my top designers using photoshop. I break it down
into three fun videos to
pull them from video one to video
two and three. On each of these pages, I resell the thing
that I’m selling on the confirmation page, and getting them
excited for the live event happening soon.
I don’t stress out about if they get all of the videos before
the webinar, or if they get some of them afterwards.
Typically it’s a second storyline outside of the webinar that
strengthens and gets them excited for it, but if they get
one of the videos after, it can still push them into making a
buying decision.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 11 --

6 | Issue No. 1
presentation who feel there is a higher perceived value of a
live event, so I’ll get
new eyeballs watching.
I learned this trick from Jay Boyer. For the readers on my lists,
I created a “Cliff Notes” version of the webinar. Basically I take
a snapshot of each of my slides with a short write-up of the
content on that slide. We send this out when there is just 24
hours left and tell them that if they haven’t had time to watch
the webinar yet, then they need to read the cliff notes ASAP
because the offer disappears soon.
Sixth, Replay Urgency Page
There are
two things that get people to buy
immediately:
Urgency and Scarcity.
Our replay page on the last day of this sequence
has to
deliver those two things. The screenshot shown on the next
page is from a funnel we built for a recent promotion with
Mike Filsaime. It helped to dramatically increase signups the
last day. Notice the elements of urgency and scarcity in the
copy on the page.
Fifth, Switching Modalities
One key to understand is that people learn things in
different ways. Some people like watching videos (or
webinars) – others like reading or listening. I know that
a big percentage of the people who didn’t show up for
the webinar got excited by the message but not the way I
delivered it, so they didn’t show up.
After the live webinar is over, I like to see what other ways I
can connect with people. Here are two examples of things
that we do:
This is a video of me giving my webinar at a live seminar.
What’s cool about this, is because I’m in front of an audience,
even though I’m giving the
same webinar, many people will
watch the entire webinar
again. It feels different even though
it’s the same content.
If I can get someone to watch my presentation twice, my
likelyhood of them buying more than doubles. It also will
attract people who don’t want to watch a powerpoint
Same Presentation, Different Delivery 1
Same Presentation, Different Delivery 2
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

lucrative and rewarding. Because the internet is constantly changing, the sites and services presented in this book may change, cease or expand with time. We hope that the skills and
knowledge acquired from this course will provide you with the ability to adapt to inevitable internet evolution. However, we cannot be held responsible for changes that may affect the
applicability of these techniques.

well as stated in any examples. If you rely upon any figures provided, you must accept the entire risk of not doing as well as the information provided.

Seventh, Promotional Sequence
So, now you have all of the pages that we use in the funnel.
Let me show you how we tie them together. Above you can
see the promotional sequence we recently did with Mike
Filsaime. We’ve tested it a dozen different ways over the past
eight months, but this sequence closes two times as many
sales from the replay as we did on the live webinar (over
$250,000 in sales in that short seven days).
We do the webinar
live on Wednesday and then again on
Friday. The rest of the promotion schedule is based around
those two live events. Monday, Tuesday and Wednesday
we promote to the registration page. We send another
email to the entire list Wednesday about an hour before
the webinar to give them one last minute push to get on.
Then, ClickFunnels is sending out three emails (one each day)
to the three indoctrination pages. Behind the scenes, either
GoToWebinar or WebinarJam is also sending out emails
reminding people before the event as well. Wednesday we
do the webinar live, then Thursday and Friday we shift back
to promoting the live event happening on Friday. We push
people through the same registration pages.
Then, after the webinar on Friday, we transition to the close.
Saturday we push out the replay, then Sunday morning we
send out the Cliff Notes with a 12 hour warning. Sunday night
we push scarcity and urgency hard with a six hour warning,
and then the campaign ends.
The Webinar Funnel
Over the past 8 months we’ve focused hard on tweaking the
presentation on the perfect webinar to double and triple
sales. By this focus on tweaking the actual funnel, we’ve seen
an additional two to three times in sales!
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 11 --

Brittany Watkins, Inner CIrCle
DCS SuCCeSS
Website
BrittanyWatkins.com
Personal Motto
Everything always
goes my way
age
34
HoMetoWn
San Francisco
eMail subscribers
25,000
incoMe earned since
Joining dcs ignite:
$750,000
VISIon BoarD Q: Can you tell us a little about your business,
why you started and how long you’ve been
an entrepreneur?
A: At the age of 15, I came home from school to find all of my
belongings on the front porch with a note that said, “You no
longer live here.” That was when my weight struggle began.
I shuffled around from friend’s house to friend’s house until
their parents started asking questions.
Being on your own at a young age does something to you...
it forces you to grow up quickly, develop serious survival
instincts, and learn to be “emotionally independent.” Lacking
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 11 --

Join Inner Circle members like Brittany by applying
online at http://ignite.dotcomsecrets.com
a consistent pillow to lay my head on at night, I knew I had to
do something fast... so I put on my game face and figured out
how to survive on my own. I found an apartment, a job and
enrolled myself in home study.
I also started making huge advances in my career. I became
one of the highest-paid executives at a large, national
bank. I bought myself a fancy car and a big house with a
view overlooking a golf course (actually, I bought three
houses...but who’s counting?). I was 24 years old, rolling
in dough, loving life, skinny and happy. I felt like I had the
world at my fingertips.
Then the real estate bubble burst—and so did my ego...
and my inflated bank account. I decided I wanted to do
something to make a difference in the world. I no longer
wanted to sell something just for the sake of a paycheck. I
wanted to do something that came from a place of love that
I had discovered within myself. In 2007, I resigned from my
corporate career and immersed myself into the exploration of
weight loss and psychotherapy.
My mission is clear and my passion is unbridled: to offer my
students a simple, science-based solution that eliminates food
cravings and emotional eating issues in minutes, not years.
The fact that this little passion brings peace, happiness and
self-love to so many other people, as it did for me, constantly
encourages me to spread the love farther and wider.
Q: What challenges were you facing when
you decided to join DCS Ignite?
The first mastermind I met another member who
taught me high ticket selling... That completely changed
my business. I had a tiny list and was able to make
$250k the first year because I used that strategy...
A: I was just starting my business and didn’t really know any
of the tips and tricks to selling online. I was doing things based
on watching other people but I didn’t have any real training...
and I didn’t have anyone to ask for help.
Q: How have Russell and DotComSecrets
helped your business grow and how has that
changed your life?
A: Honestly, it was more about the people I met in the group.
Russell is amazing and has great ideas. The first mastermind I
met another member who taught me high ticket selling and
I’ve been using that model ever since.
That completely changed my business. I had a tiny list and was
able to make $250k the first year because I used that strategy...
Now I use Russell’s script for cold calling and it’s really blowing
me away. It’s so easy to close people in a way that doesn’t feel
sleazy or even salesy.
Q: Can you share a favorite accomplishment
you’ve made as a business owner?
A: Wow, making my first $64,000 in one launch was definitely
a high point. Proving the system - that I could have an online
business - was a day I will never forget.
Q: What is your top goal for the rest of 2015?
A: My goal for 2015 is to hit $1 million in revenue.
